item.title: Protracted Iran War Risks Pushing UK Into Recession, NIESR Says
item.description: The UK could slip into recession later this year and be forced to hike interest rates aggressively to contain inflation if the Middle East crisis worsens, the National Institute of Economic and Social Research warned.

item.title: UAE Exit Blindsides OPEC and Threatens to Shake Its Grip on Oil
item.description: The shock decision by the United Arab Emirates to quit OPEC blindsided its partners of six decades. Now the cartel will have to fight to stay relevant in a fast-shifting global oil market.

item.title: Gold Steady After Two-Day Drop as Iran War Fans Inflation Fears
item.description: Gold steadied after a two-day drop as investor focus remained on talks between the US and Iran, with the indefinite closure of the Strait of Hormuz continuing to heighten inflation risks.

item.title: Banxico Considers Ending Easing Cycle With One Last Rate Cut
item.description: Mexico’s central bank will consider one more interest rate cut at its May meeting that may bring an end to the easing cycle it started two years ago, Governor Victoria Rodriguez Ceja said Tuesday.

item.title: Bank Groups Eye Changes to Fed’s Relaxed Capital Proposals
item.description: Bank groups said that although the Federal Reserve’s eased capital plans are a major improvement over previous versions, the recent proposals still need changes to help avoid risk assessments they say may hinder banks’ ability to boost lending.

item.title: Vale Lags Expectations as Cost Pressures Offset Price Gain
item.description: The world’s top iron-ore producer Vale SA posted disappointing first-quarter results as operational pressures more than offset higher prices and volumes.

item.title: Mondelez Chief Sees US Consumer Confidence Weakening on Iran War
item.description: Mondelez International Inc.’s top executive sees the war in Iran eroding consumer confidence as Americans worry about the economy, job security and affordability.

item.title: Asia Open Clouded by US Stock Slide on AI Worries: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia are set for a subdued start after a tech-led selloff dragged down US benchmarks, as doubts resurface over whether massive spending in artificial intelligence will pay off — just as traders brace for a wave of megacap earnings.

item.title: Oil Holds Gains as Traders Focus on Next Steps for Peace Talks
item.description: Oil steadied as investors focused on the next steps for peace talks over the Iran war, with the crucial Strait of Hormuz remaining effectively closed, extending disruptions that have upended global markets.

item.title: Canada’s Finance Minister Gets a Revenue Boost and Decides to Use It
item.description: Plus: Barrick picks New York

item.title: Samsung Dynasty’s Wealth Doubles to $45 Billion in Just One Year
item.description: When Lee Kun-hee, the patriarch behind Samsung Electronics Co. died in 2020, his dynasty soon dealt with a crisis on two fronts: first, a multi-billion-dollar inheritance tax. The following year, his son Jay Y. Lee was jailed after being convicted of bribing South Korea’s former President Park Geun-hye to win support for his succession.

item.title: Nvidia-Tied Data Center Raises $4.59 Billion From Junk-Bond Sale
item.description: A Nevada data center project tied to Nvidia Corp. has raised $4.59 billion from a junk-bond sale, adding to a wave of deals to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: North Korea’s Nuclear Program Reaches Tipping Point for the US
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, how North Korea’s rapid missile buildup is challenging US defenses and reshaping regional ties.

item.title: Mexico Says US Intelligence Aided in Capture of Cartel Boss
item.description: Mexico leaned on US intelligence to arrest an alleged cartel boss with a $5 million bounty on his head, although officials stressed that no foreign agents were on the ground for the operation.

item.title: Booking’s 2Q Guidance Misses on Middle East Impact, Shares Slide
item.description: Booking Holdings Inc reported worse-than-expected second-quarter guidance for revenue as the Middle East conflict is expected to impact travel demand through the end of June.

item.title: Bond Traders Ramp Up Wagers Hedging for 5% Yields as Oil Surges
item.description: Traders in the Treasury options market are bracing for long-dated bond yields to surge past 5% as a rally in oil prices continues unabated.

item.title: NXP Rallies After Stronger Auto Market Bolsters Forecast
item.description: NXP Semiconductors NV jumped in late trading after giving an upbeat revenue forecast, a sign the chipmaker is bouncing back from a prolonged auto industry slump and tariff uncertainties.

item.title: Kone Is Said to Near €29 Billion Deal for Rival TK Elevator
item.description: Kone Oyj is nearing a cash-and-stock deal to acquire TK Elevator, people familiar with the matter said, in what will be one of the biggest-ever private equity exits in Europe.

item.title: China’s Used Cooking Oil Ships to US as War Drives Biofuel Boom
item.description: American imports of used cooking oil from China are set to accelerate as increased US biofuel-blending requirements kick in and the Iran war drives up energy costs, making the feedstock a relative bargain.

item.title: Carney Eyes Building Boom as Oil Prices Lift Revenues in Canada
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney chose to spend a revenue windfall on measures to support building major export-boosting projects, leaving Canada’s fiscal trajectory little changed.

item.title: US Warns of Sanctions Risks for Chinese Refiners of Iranian Oil
item.description: The US warned financial institutions of sanction risks on Chinese oil refineries over ties to Iran, ramping up economic pressure on Tehran and risking tension with Beijing ahead of an expected summit between the country’s leaders.

item.title: Italy’s Eni Expands Venezuela Push With Orinoco Oil Deal
item.description: Eni SpA agreed to restart an oil project in Venezuela’s Orinoco Belt, deepening its presence as US sanctions ease.

item.title: Hollywood Gear Supplier Quixote to Exit Atlanta Operations
item.description: Quixote, a supplier of gear to movie and TV productions, is shutting down its Atlanta-area operations and exiting many of its soundstages in Los Angeles in the latest sign of the slump in US entertainment filming.

item.title: Appalachian Lithium Cache Enough to Power 130 Million EVs, USGS Says
item.description: The Appalachian region of the eastern US holds enough lithium to curb America’s reliance on imports for centuries, according to new research by the US Geological Survey, underscoring domestic resource potential as demand for critical minerals accelerates.

item.title: TD Securities’ Nixon Leaves for National Bank Fixed Income Sales
item.description: Katy Nixon, a director in debt capital markets syndication at TD Securities, is leaving the bank for a fixed income sales role at National Bank of Canada, according to a spokesperson at National Bank.

item.title: Oaktree Capital Accuses BJ’s of Reneging on Tariff Refund Trade
item.description: Oaktree Capital Management LP sued BJ’s Wholesale Club Inc. for allegedly backing out of a deal to sell its rights to around $29 million in tariff refunds for 70 cents on the dollar.

item.title: Jane Street Set to Double Footprint in London Office Move
item.description: Jane Street Group has placed an offer to lease a new London office, a deal that will see the rapidly growing trading firm almost double its footprint in the UK capital.

item.title: Google Allows Pentagon to Use Its AI in Classified Military Work
item.description: Google has reached an agreement with the US Defense Department to allow its artificial intelligence systems to be used for classified military work, according to a Pentagon official, a deal that came together as researchers at the company protested against it.

item.title: Musk Lawyer Says Altman Made ‘Mockery’ of OpenAI Public Mission
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawyer argued to a jury Tuesday that the billionaire’s work to develop artificial intelligence technology for the public good was undermined by the co-founders of OpenAI when they turned it into a profit-making venture.

item.title: AI Boom to Triple US Power Equipment Market to $65 Billion
item.description: US spending on power-plant equipment is expected to triple through 2030, primarily driven by the growth of data centers that may account for as much as 40% of total investment, according to a report Tuesday from Wood Mackenzie Ltd.
